title: Command Recognition
description: This block can be used to extract values from any text. The search pattern is defined in the properties.

properties.description: Characters used to extract a value: \v = value, \1 = Byte interpreted as 1st byte of the output value (\2, \3, ...), \h = value interpreted as hexadecimal number Characters to navigate through the text: \. = any character, \w = any word, \# = any number, \d = digits 0-9, \m = character A-Z/a-z/0-9, \a = character A-Z/a-z, \s12 = skip 12 characters, \iText\i = jump to 'Text' Special characters: \x = Hexadecimal number (e.g. 0x09), \\ = Slash, \t = Tab (0x09), \b = Space (0x02) or Tab (0x09), \r = Return (0x0d), \n = Newline (0x0a)
If checked, the values \1, \2, \3, etc. are used with algebraic sign in command recognition (Signed Integer).
